name: Reliable Transfer with Sliding Window
description: Sliding-window ARQ provides reliable, in-order delivery over an unreliable channel: the sender segments data and transmits a window of packets while running timers; arriving ACKs slide the window and admit new packets, while timeouts trigger retransmission of unacknowledged segments, continuing until all data is delivered.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Sender data stream"]
    N2["Segment into packets"]
    N3["Send window of packets"]
    N4["Await ACKs (timers)"]
    N5{"ACK in time?"}
    N6["Slide window, send new"]
    N7["Retransmit unacked"]
    N8{"All data delivered?"}
    N9["Continue"]
    N10["Reliable in-order delivery"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|yes| N6
    N5 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|resend| N4
    N6 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|next window| N3
    N8 -->|yes| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Computer Networks (5th ed.)
